questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. The sudden resignation of the CEO threw the entire company into complete chaos.
questions.explanation: A) Chaos(noun) – Disorder, confusion, anarchy, mayhem, turmoil.अव्यवस्था / अफरातफरी Antonym: Order(noun) – A condition in which everything is arranged properly, harmony, regulation.व्यवस्था / अनुशासन •Mayhem(noun) – Violent disorder, chaos, turmoil.हिंसा / कोलाहल •Anarchy(noun) – Absence of government or law, lawlessness.अराजकता •Turmoil(noun) – A state of great disturbance, confusion, or uncertainty.उथल-पुथल

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence Honesty is a quality that forms part of your character or personality, reflecting how genuine and trustworthy you are in all your interactions.
questions.explanation: C)Trait(noun) – A quality or characteristic that forms part of a person's nature or personality.विशेषता

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. She speaks / so soft that / hardly anyone / can hear her.
questions.explanation: B) 'so soft that' के बदले 'so softly that' का use होगा क्योंकि यहाँ Verb 'speaks' के ढंग (manner) को दर्शाने के लिए Adverb 'softly' की आवश्यकता है, Adjective 'soft' नहीं। Adverb का use Verb को modify करने के लिए होता है; जैसे— She sings beautifully. 'so softly that' will be used instead of 'so soft that' because here, to show the manner of the verb 'speaks', the adverb 'softly' is required, not the adjective 'soft'. Adverb is used to modify the verb; Like— She sings beautifully.

questions.question: Select the best express Antonym of the given italicize word. The hikers struggled through the rugged terrain, where every step tested their endurance.
questions.explanation: A) Rugged (adjective)– Rough, uneven, harsh, craggy, jagged.उबड़-खाबड़ Antonym:Smooth (adjective)– Having an even and regular surface, flat, polished, level.चिकना / समतल •Jagged (adjective)– Sharp, pointed, irregular.नुकीला •Uneven (adjective)– Not level or smooth, irregular, bumpy.असमतल •Craggy (adjective)– Rocky, steep, rough.चट्टानों से भरा हुआ

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph A) This category comprises a highly disproportionate and distressing 25.6 per cent of all suicide victims in 2021, as per the latest report of the National Crime Records Bureau. B) Their pitiable state is further highlighted by the fact that the graph of daily wagers taking their own lives has steadily risen in the past few years — from 15,735 in 2014 to 37,666 in 2020 and 42,004 in 2021. C) This shocking trend, sadly, underscores an acute paucity of empathy for the poor and marginalised sections. D) The suicide statistics in the country reflect the poignant story of daily-wage earners giving out a cry for help.
questions.explanation: A) DABC D:This sentence sets up the context by introducing the topic of suicide statistics among daily-wage earners in the country. A:This sentence then provides specific statistical data, stating that daily-wage earners represent a significant percentage of all suicide victims in 2021 according to a particular report. This directly builds on the general introduction provided in sentence D. B:Following the specific data given in sentence A, sentence B then provides additional historical data, showing how the situation has changed over the years. C:Lastly, sentence C offers a commentary or analysis on this situation, concluding the paragraph with an emotional appeal and indictment of society's lack of empathy. This makes sense as a closing sentence as it gives the reader something to think about based on the facts presented in the previous sentences.

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. I still / do not understand / why he did not / reply my email.
questions.explanation: A) इस वाक्य में "reply my email" के स्थान पर "reply to my email" का प्रयोग होगा। ऐसा इसलिए क्योंकि "reply" एक intransitive verb है और इसके object से पहले preposition "to" का उपयोग किया जाता है। Correct: He replied to my question. In this sentence, "reply my email" should be replaced with "reply to my email." This is because "reply" is an intransitive verb, and it requires the preposition "to" before the object. The verb "reply" is intransitive, meaning it cannot take a direct object. Correct: He replied to my question.

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option to fill in the blank. Though Pradeep _____________ for Jamaica, he landed up in Nigeria
questions.explanation: C) 'Set out' का use होगा क्योंकि "set out" का अर्थ होता है किसी निश्चित स्थल या दिशा में प्रस्थान करना। Sentence में mention किया गया है कि Pradeep Jamaica के लिए प्रस्थान किया, लेकिन वह Nigeria में पहुँच गया, इसलिए 'set out' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Set aside' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को अलग रखना, 'Set about' का अर्थ है शुरुआत करना या किसी कार्य में लग जाना, और 'Set up' का अर्थ है स्थापित करना या तैयार करना, जो इस context में सही नहीं है।

questions.question: Select the INCORRECTLY spelt word
questions.explanation: C) The correct spelling of ‘Apraisal’is ‘Appraisal’, which means “an act of assessing someone or something” or “a formal assessment of value, quality, or performance.” मूल्यांकन, आकलन

questions.question: Select the most appropriate option that can substitute the underlined segment in the given sentence. Whoever got the few answers correct has to leave the room.
questions.explanation: D) 'Fewest' का use होगा क्योंकि "fewest" का अर्थ होता है सबसे कम (संख्यात्मक रूप से)। Sentence में mention किया गया है कि जिसने सबसे कम सही उत्तर दिए हैं, उसे कमरा छोड़ना होगा, इसलिए 'fewest' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'fewer' का अर्थ है कम (तुलनात्मक), 'most few' गलत grammatical structure है, और 'more fewer' भी असंगत है क्योंकि 'more' और 'fewer' एक साथ नहीं आ सकते। 'Fewest' will be used because it means "the least in number." The sentence states that whoever got the least number of correct answers has to leave the room, making 'fewest' correct here. Whereas, 'fewer' means "less" (comparative), 'most few' is grammatically incorrect, and 'more fewer' is illogical since 'more' and 'fewer' cannot be used together

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. There are others waiting to grab an opportunity; if you don’t act fast, you are going to ________.
questions.explanation: A)A miss the boat(idiom) - to lose an opportunity to do something because you were too slow to act.अवसर गवाना

questions.question: Select the option that rectifies the spelling of the underlined word in the given sentence. A hospital's hygine is the standard against which cleanliness is measured.
questions.explanation: C) The correct spelling of ‘hygine’is ‘hygiene’, which means “the conditions or practices of maintaining cleanliness to preserve health.” स्वच्छता, स्वास्थ्य-रक्षा संबंधी स्वच्छ आदतें।

questions.question: Select the word which means the same as the group of words underlined in the given sentence During the monsoon season, there was a _____________ downpour, with rain falling rapidly and in copious quantities, flooding the streets within minutes.
questions.explanation: A)Torrential(adjective) – Relating to or resembling a very heavy downpour of rain मूसलधार

questions.question: Select the most appropriate idiom to fill in the blank. I think we should ________ before buying this car.
questions.explanation: A)Hold our horses(idiom) – To be patient and not act impulsively or hastily.धैर्य बरतना

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. The alarming rise in sea levels has prompted global leaders to convene an emergency climate summit.
questions.explanation: D) Alarming(adjective) – Disturbing, Worrying, Frightening, Shocking, Startling चिंताजनक Synonym:Worrying(adjective) – causing anxiety or concern, alarming. चिंताजनक Extrinsic(adjective) – External, coming from outside, not inherent. बाहरी, बाह्य Outward(adjective) – Directed or moving toward the outside; external. बाहरी Amicable(adjective) – Friendly, peaceable. दोस्ताना

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains an error. Stephan, however, contrived / to put away / his departure / for eighteen months.
questions.explanation: A) 'to put away' के बदले'to put off'का use होगा क्योंकि 'put off' का अर्थ होता है 'टालना' या 'स्थगित करना', जबकि 'put away' का अर्थ 'संग्रह करना' या 'रख देना' होता है, जो यहाँ गलत है; जैसे— Stephan, however, contrived to put off his departure for eighteen months. 'to put off' will be used instead of 'to put away' because 'put off' means 'to postpone,' which is the correct meaning in this context; Like— Stephan, however, contrived to put off his departure for eighteen months.

questions.question: Select the best express synonym of the given underlined word. His skills are manifold, ranging from classical music composition to advanced mathematical calculations.
questions.explanation: A) Manifold(adjective) – Multiple, Numerous, Diverse, Varied, Multifaceted विविध Synonym:Numerous(adjective) – great in number, many, manifold. बहुत Intriguing(adjective) – Arousing curiosity or interest; fascinating. दिलचस्प Austere(adjective) – Severe, strict, plain, or simple in manner, attitude, or lifestyle. कठोर, सादगीपूर्ण Adventurous(adjective) – Willing to take risks or try out new things; daring. साहसी

questions.question: Select the most appropriate collocating word to fill in the blank. The sheer number of visitors is ______________ a threat to the area
questions.explanation: A) 'Posing' का use होगा क्योंकि "posing" का अर्थ होता है किसी खतरे या समस्या को प्रस्तुत करना या सामने लाना। Sentence में mention किया गया है कि अधिक संख्या में पर्यटकों के आने से क्षेत्र को खतरा हो रहा है, इसलिए 'posing' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'Presenting' का अर्थ है प्रस्तुत करना, 'Causing' का अर्थ है कारण बनना, और 'Giving rise to' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ को उत्तेजित करना या जन्म देना, जो इस context में सही नहीं है।

questions.question: The following sentence has been split into four segments. Identify the segment that contains a grammatical error. You know that I am fond of reading / and also that all books are my favourite / but you don’t know that this is one of / my favourite book.
options: and also that all books are my favourite
but you don’t know that this is one of
you know that I am fond of reading
questions.explanation: A) ‘book' के बदले'books'का use होगा क्योंकि ‘this is one of’ के बाद उस Noun को Plural में use किया जाता है; जैसे— This is one of my favourite books. 'books'will be used instead of 'book' because after 'this is one of' the Noun is always used in Plural form; Like— This is one of my favourite books.

questions.question: Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph A) Paved Paradise: How Parking Explains the World by Henry Grabar is a history of how increased space for cars has shaped our cities, clogged our streets and fouled the air. B) The root of the word ‘parking’ lies in curbside bits of greenery, the tiny parks where cars were stationed in the early days of automobiles. C) How far it’s come from those placid beginnings. D) If driving is freedom and motion, parking is its cramped, illtempered partner.
questions.explanation: A) BCDA B:This sentence provides a starting point, introducing the original concept of "parking" as it was associated with the early days of automobiles. It sets the historical context and serves as the most logical opening sentence C:This sentence connects to B by expressing the evolution of the concept from the initial definition mentioned in B D:This sentence continues the narrative from C, contrasting the notion of driving with parking and adding a bit more to our understanding of how the concept of "parking" has evolved. A:This final sentence culminates the discussion on the evolution of parking, linking it to a specific book which discusses this issue further, illustrating its impact on our cities and environment.

questions.question: Identify the option that arranges the given parts in the correct order to form a meaningful paragraph. I knew that the remedy lay not A) Let the past go. Be at ease. Allow the future B) Rohan had advised me over and again: C) to arrive at its own pace D) in finding a new flower but in what unfurling its secrets when it will.
questions.explanation: A) DBAC D starts the sentence by introducing the problem: "in finding a new flower but in what unfurling its secrets when it will." This sets the context for the remedy that follows. B follows D as it introduces Rohan's advice: "Rohan had advised me over and again." This connects logically because the advice relates to the problem mentioned in D. A comes next as it presents the core advice: "Let the past go. Be at ease. Allow the future." This follows B because it elaborates on what Rohan advised. C concludes the sentence by completing the thought: "to arrive at its own pace." This connects grammatically with A because "Allow the future" naturally leads to "to arrive at its own pace," forming a meaningful infinitive phrase. Explanation in Hindi: D sentence की शुरुआत करता है, क्योंकि यह समस्या को introduce करता है: "नया फूल ढूँढने में नहीं, बल्कि उसके रहस्यों को उसके समय पर खुलने देने में।" यह उपाय के लिए context देता है। B, D के बाद आता है, क्योंकि यह रोहन की सलाह बताता है: "रोहन ने बार-बार मुझे समझाया था।" यह D से जुड़ता है क्योंकि सलाह समस्या से related है। A, B के बाद आता है, क्योंकि यह मुख्य सलाह देता है: "अतीत को जाने दो। आराम करो। भविष्य को अपने समय पर आने दो।" यह B का विस्तार है। C sentence को पूरा करता है: "अपनी गति से आने दो।" यह A से grammatically जुड़ता है, क्योंकि "भविष्य को आने दो" का स्वाभाविक continuation "अपनी गति से आने दो" है।

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Q21. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 1.
questions.explanation: B) Catches'का use होगा क्योंकि यहाँ पर 'a personality or character of the story' subject singular है, इसलिए singular verb 'catches' का use करना चाहिए। 'Catch' plural form है, 'Caught' past form है, और 'Has caught' present perfect form है जो इस context में सही नहीं है।Catches'should be used because the subject 'a personality or character of the story' is singular, and hence the singular verb 'catches' is appropriate. 'Catch' is a plural form, 'Caught' is a past form, and 'Has caught' is in the present perfect tense, which doesn't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Q22. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 2.
questions.explanation: C) 'So'का use होगा क्योंकि वाक्य में कारण-परिणाम संबंध दिखाया जा रहा है: किसी कहानी के पात्र आमतौर पर बच्चे की रुचि को सबसे ज्यादा आकर्षित करते हैं, इसलिए उन पात्रों को जीवंत करने के लिए विभिन्न आवाज़ें उपयोग की जानी चाहिए। ‘So'should be used because the sentence is showing a cause-and-effect relationship: the characters of a story usually attract a child's interest, therefore, different voices should be used to animate those characters.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Q23. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 3.
questions.explanation: B) For'का use होगा क्योंकि sentence में बताया जा रहा है कि प्रत्येक character के लिए अलग-अलग आवाज़ या शैली का use किया जा सकता है। 'For' इस context में प्रत्येक पात्र के लिए विशेषता या आवाज़ देने का संदर्भ प्रदान करता है, इसलिए 'for' यहाँ सही है। जबकि 'With' का अर्थ है साथ में, 'From' का अर्थ है किसी चीज़ से, और 'By' का अर्थ है किसी के द्वारा, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'For'should be used because the sentence is suggesting using different styles or voices specifically tailored for each character. 'For' provides a reference to the specificity or voice designated for each character, making 'for' appropriate here. Whereas, 'With' implies alongside or in possession of, 'From' implies a point of origin, and 'By' denotes by means of, which don't fit in this context.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Q24. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 4.
questions.explanation: C) 'Parts'का use होगा क्योंकि जब किसी कहानी को सुनाया जाता है, तो उसमें विभिन्न 'parts' या घटनाक्रम होते हैं जिन्हें act out किया जा सकता है। 'Sheets' और 'Pages' का अर्थ है कहानी की शारीरिक तरह की पृष्ठ, जो इस context में सही नहीं है। 'Words' का अर्थ है वाक्यांश या शब्द, जिसे act out करना संभव नहीं है। Parts'should be used because when narrating a story, there are various 'parts' or events that can be acted out. 'Sheets' and 'Pages' refer to the physical pages of the story, which doesn't make sense in this context. 'Words' means phrases or words, which aren't feasible to act out. Therefore, 'parts' is the most fitting choice for this blank.

questions.question: Comprehension: In the following passage, some words have been deleted. Read the passage carefully and select the most appropriate option to fill in each blank. How should one read a story to a child? Usually, a personality or character of the story (1) ______ the child’s interest the most. (2) ______, use different voices (3) ______ each character or act out various (4) ______ from the story, to bring life to those characters. (5) ______ out parts from the story will make story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. Q25. Select the most appropriate option to fill in blank number 5.
questions.explanation: B) Acting'का use होगा क्योंकि उस समय स्टोरी के पात्रों को जीवन देने के लिए कथा के विभिन्न भागों का अभिनय किया जाता है। इसलिए 'Acting' out parts से आशय है कि बच्चों के लिए कथा सत्र मजेदार और यादगार बन जाएगा। 'Telling', 'Asking', और 'Saying' इस context में सही नहीं हैं क्योंकि वे स्टोरी के पात्रों को जीवन देने का अभिप्रेत अर्थ नहीं देते। 'Acting'should be used because it refers to portraying different parts of the story to bring the characters to life. Hence, 'Acting' out parts implies making the story sessions fun and memorable for the kids. 'Telling', 'Asking', and 'Saying' don't convey the intended meaning of bringing the characters to life in this context.
